titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/241

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=210 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-02 13:02:56.701546+00 | translation moa=211 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-02 13:02:56.671561+00

p.cl: Omnibus sancte martris Ecclesie filiis ⁊ fidelibus . Seier de Quinci Comes . Winton’ . Salutem.

Nouerit uniuersitas uestra me in
presentia domini regis Scott’ ⁊ optimatum suorum in plenaria Curia sua apud forfar quietum clamasse pro me omnibus
heredibus meis abbati Ecclesie sancte crucis de Edenburc ⁊ Conuentu eiusdem domus in perpetuum quicquid iuris habere uidebar in
Ecclesia de Trauernent ⁊ eius pertinentiis diuine remunerationis intuitu ⁊ pro salute dominorum meorum Regum Scott’ ⁊ pre
decessorum ⁊ successorum meorum . Preterea confirmo eis unam Carucatam terre ⁊ decem acras in territorio de Trauernent
apud faueside cum libero exitu ⁊ communi pastura quam pater meus eis in liberam ⁊ perpetuam Elemosinam dedit
sicut Carta sua eis testatur . Confirmo Etiam Eis totam illam terram in nodrif quam pater meus eis dedit ad
seruiendum Capelle eiusdem uille . Tenend’ sibi in liberam ⁊ puram Elemosinam per easdem diuisas per quas pater meus in
presentia proborum uirorum ipsos Canonicos inde saisiauit.

Teste Domino Rege . Comite Dauid . fratre domini Regis . Rob’
de London’ . Willelmo Cumin . Florent’ . Cancell’ . Willelmo de Bosco . Willelmo ⁊ Oliuero . Capellanis domini regis . Hugone
de prebenda . Herberto de Camera . A . persona de Kingor . A . de Hasteng’ ⁊ aliis quam pluribus

div.p: To all sons and faithful of Holy Mother Church, Saer de Qunicy, earl of Winchester, Greeting: The whole body of you should know that in the presence of the lord king of Scots and his magnates in his full court at Forfar I quitclaimed for myself and all my heirs to the abbot of the church of Holyrood in Edinburgh and the convent of that house in perpetuity whatever right I am considered to have in the church of Tranent and its pertinents, moved by divine reward and for the welfare of the soul of my lords the kings of Scots and my predecessors and successors. Moreover, I confirm to them one ploughgate of land and ten acres in the territory of Tranent at Falside, with free exit and common pasture, which my father gave them in free and perpetual alms, as his charter to them attests. I also confirm to them the whole land in Niddrie which my father gave them to serve the chapel of the same toun. For them to hold in free and perpetual alms, by the same bounds with which my father saised the canons themselves in the presence of worthy men. Witness the Lord King, Earl David the brother of the lord King, Robert of London, Wiliam Comyn, Florence the chancellor, William del Bois, William and Oliver the king’s chaplains, Hugh de Prebenda, Herbert de Camera, Adam the parson of Kinghorn, Adam of Hastings, and very many others.
